title: aquilegia formosa var. hypolasia
intro: Aquilegia formosa var. hypolasia is a lesser-known variety of the Western columbine plant, native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities, making it useful for treating respiratory issues and wounds. The health benefits of Aquilegia formosa var. hypolasia include relieving congestion, reducing fever, and promoting healing, which can be attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
